title: EVA chronicles illustrations
wikitext:
[]: EVA chronicles illustrations             Quote Post by xdiesp » Wed Mar 23, 2011 1:35 am [url]http://www.evangelion-not-end.ru/artbooks/1533-evangelion-chronicle-illustrations[/url] Sorry if old. I was checking this art book which I'm not sure is internets fan work, or an actually published thing. This one sparked my interest in particular: art books showed how the evas look without armor, but in here you can see the holes beneath the breastplate. I don't think it was shown when absorbing zeruel. [url]http://s212.photobucket.com/albums/cc123/xdiesp/?action=view&current=Pag34.jpg[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Mar 23, 2011 7:04 am It wasn't shown with holes beneath the breastplate when it was eating Zeruel because that part was still on. The holes underneath were briefly revealed when the armor burst off, however. (I can't recall if this part was exposed in Episode 20 or covered by bandages)

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Jul 16, 2012 4:38 pm I've been thinking about getting this book. For those who have it what are your thoughts? I'm also interested in Carmine by Sadamoto. Don't know though if these 2 can be compared since that is his illustrations vs. other types of illustrations of Chronicles.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Jul 16, 2012 5:50 pm Evangelion Chronicle isn't a book. It's a set of 30+ magazines that were sold separately and released in regular intervals. It's possible to maybe find someone selling the entire thing on ebay, in a binder or something, but it probably isn't going to be cheap.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Jul 16, 2012 6:02 pm It looks like the artwork was compiled into art books then? This is what the OP was asking about (apologies for the URLs). Original: [url]http://www.amazon.co.jp/%E3%82%A8%E3%83%B4%E3%82%A1%E3%83%B3%E3%82%B2%E3%83%AA%E3%82%AA%E3%83%B3%E3%83%BB%E3%82%AF%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%AF%E3%83%AB%E3%82%A4%E3%83%A9%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88%E3%83%AC%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B7%E3%83%A7%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BA/dp/486332619X/ref=sr_1_4?ie=UTF8&qid=1342479558&sr=8-4[/url] A revision came out in 2011: [url]http://www.amazon.co.jp/%E3%82%A8%E3%83%B4%E3%82%A1%E3%83%B3%E3%82%B2%E3%83%AA%E3%82%AA%E3%83%B3%E3%83%BB%E3%82%AF%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%AF%E3%83%AB-%E3%82%A4%E3%83%A9%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88%E3%83%AC%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B7%E3%83%A7%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BA-%E6%96%B0%E8%A8%82%E7%89%88/dp/4863323646/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1342479558&sr=8-1[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 19, 2012 2:36 am Correct. The Asuka version has everything from the Rei version (including insert) with added material from the new movies. They're very pretty books. If anyone is interested in the Rei version I'm looking to sell one; PM me if interested. Sadamoto's Carmine is a very different book. It only contains his work, and it includes work from other anime series (The Girl Who Leapt Through Time, etc.). I got both books this past weekend and consider both to be good purchases.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Thu Jul 19, 2012 12:05 pm After Der Mond and Die Sterne I'm opening to having an art book with Asuka on the cover for a change.
